source_paper_title: On Time-Consistent Equilibrium Stopping Under Aggregation of Diverse Discount Rates
context:
Section 5.1 (Geometric Brownian motion), Remark 5.3, page 28 (PDF page 28/38 in the provided parsed text)

As a sharp contrast, in our framework with a general attitude function \(\varphi(x)\), it is an appealing problem to find a general characterization of the optimal equilibrium if it exists, which will be left for our future research.
title: General characterization of the optimal equilibrium in time-inconsistent stopping under nonlinear aggregation of diverse discount rates
background_latex:
- Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb P)\) support a one-dimensional time-homogeneous diffusion \(X^x=(X^x_t)_{t\ge0}\) with \(X^x_0=x\in\mathbb R\), and let \(\mathbb F=(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge0}\) be the filtration generated by \(X^x\).
- Let \(\mathcal T\) be the set of all \(\mathbb F\)-stopping times.
- Let \(\rho\) be a nonnegative random variable (discount rate) with distribution function \(F_\rho\) on \([0,\infty)\), independent of \(X^x\).
- Let \(g:\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\) be a measurable payoff function, and let \(\varphi:\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\) be a strictly increasing aggregation attitude function.
- For a Borel set \(R\subset\mathbb R\) (a stopping region), define the hitting time \(\tau(x,R):=\inf\{t>0: X^x_t\in R\}\).
- Define the aggregated continuation value
\[J(x,R):=\int_0^\infty \varphi\Big(\mathbb E\big[e^{-r\tau(x,R)}\,g(X^x_{\tau(x,R)})\big]\Big)\,dF_\rho(r).\]
- Define the policy-improvement operator \(\Theta\) by
\[\Theta(R):=\{x\in\mathbb R: \varphi(g(x))>J(x,R)\}\cup R.\]
- An equilibrium (also called a mild equilibrium in the paper) is a Borel set \(R\) such that \(\Theta(R)=R\). Let \(\mathcal E\) denote the set of all such equilibria.
- For \(R\in\mathcal E\), define the associated equilibrium value function
\[V(x,R):=\varphi(g(x))\vee J(x,R),\qquad x\in\mathbb R.\]
- An optimal equilibrium is an equilibrium \(R^{\mathrm{opt}}\in\mathcal E\) satisfying \(V(x,R^{\mathrm{opt}})\ge V(x,R)\) for all \(x\in\mathbb R\) and all \(R\in\mathcal E\).
problem_definition_latex:
Find a general characterization (and verifiable existence conditions) for an optimal equilibrium \(R^{\mathrm{opt}}\in\mathcal E\) for the time-inconsistent stopping problem with aggregated preference
\[\sup_{\tau\in\mathcal T}\int_0^\infty \varphi\Big(\mathbb E\big[e^{-r\tau}g(X^x_\tau)\big]\Big)\,dF_\rho(r),\]
in the regime where \(\varphi\) is a general strictly increasing attitude function (not assumed to satisfy the sufficient condition C-(iii) in the paper). In particular, characterize \(R^{\mathrm{opt}}\) in terms of \(X^x\), \(g\), \(F_\rho\), and \(\varphi\), or provide necessary and sufficient conditions under which such an \(R^{\mathrm{opt}}\) exists and is unique.

\[\sup_{\tau\in\mathcal T}\int_0^\infty \varphi\Big(\mathbb E\big[e^{-r\tau}g(X^x_\tau)\big]\Big)\,dF_\rho(r),\]

In particular, characterize \(R^{\mathrm{opt}}\) in terms of \(X^x\), \(g\), \(F_\rho\), and \(\varphi\), or provide necessary and sufficient conditions under which such an \(R^{\mathrm{opt}}\) exists and is unique.
